page: Amazon Web Services m7g.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores on the 64-bit ARMv8 architecture, utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the m7g.xlarge in the average tier for most general computing tasks. It demonstrates stable single-core and multi-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests, alongside average memory bandwidth speeds, reaching up to 386.52 GB/sec for cached read-only small blocks. However, the architecture shows performance trade-offs in specific workloads. It scores in the weak or poor categories for cryptographic operations, bzip3 decompression, and LLM text generation, where the 7B model achieves only 6.03 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture provides dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading, offering predictable resource allocation for general-purpose workloads. The instance is well-suited for applications such as static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 8.15 GB/sec, Redis database operations, and software compilation. It is less suitable for heavy cryptographic tasks, complex ray tracing, or large language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7g.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores running at 2.6 GHz and 16.0 GB of system memory, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average performance for general computing, software compilation, and static web serving, alongside high memory bandwidth for cached operations. However, it exhibits weaker performance in cryptographic tasks, bzip3 decompression, and LLM inference. This server is best suited for balanced workloads such as Redis deployments, web serving, and general application hosting where dedicated ARM resources offer qualitative cost-efficiency.

tagline: A general-purpose ARM64 virtual server featuring dedicated physical cores and balanced memory allocation for predictable application hosting.
